task.title: Configuring a static route by using nmcli

li.p: The LAN connection profile exists and it configures this host to be in the same IP subnet as the gateways.

context.p: You can add a static route to an existing NetworkManager connection profile using the nmcli connection modify command.
The procedure below configures the following routes:

li.p: An IPv4 route to the remote 198.51.100.0/24 network. The corresponding gateway with the IP address 192.0.2.10 is reachable through the LAN connection profile.
An IPv6 route to the remote 2001:db8:2::/64 network. The corresponding gateway with the IP address 2001:db8:1::10 is reachable through the LAN connection profile.

step.cmd: Add the static IPv4 route to the LAN connection profile:

info.p: To set multiple routes in one step, pass the individual routes comma-separated to the command:

step.cmd: Add the static IPv6 route to the LAN connection profile:

li.codeblock: # ip -6 route ... 2001:db8:2::/64 via 2001:db8:1::10 dev enp1s0 metric 1024 pref medium
